name: Alex Rivera
backstory: I graduated from MIT with a Computer Science degree in 2013. I started my career at a small startup in Boston where I learned full-stack development. In 2016, I joined Google as a Software Engineer on the Search Infrastructure team, where I worked on large-scale distributed systems. After 5 years at Google, I moved to a fintech startup as a Senior Engineer and eventually became Tech Lead. I'm passionate about clean code, mentoring junior developers, and building scalable systems. Outside of work, I contribute to open-source projects and enjoy hiking on weekends. I've spoken at 3 tech conferences about microservices architecture. My biggest career achievement was leading the redesign of our payment processing system that handled $2B in transactions. I learned Rust in 2020 and fell in love with its memory safety guarantees. I'm currently exploring AI/ML integration in backend systems.
